REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS SCOTT FRIEDMAN, REBECCA KADEN'S RENEWED MOTION TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 22, 2019 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN, REBECCA KADEN NOTICE OF MOTION AND RENEWED MOTION TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY DENIED THE MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND EXPRESSLY WITHOUT PREJUDICE PROVIDING PLAINTIFF WITH OPPORTUNITY TO ADDRESS THE ISSUES AS LISTED IN THE ORDER. PLAINTIFF HAS DONE SO AND MAY FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. DAVIS-STERLING ACT DOES NOT APPLY TO THE NEWLY PROPOSED CAUSES OF ACTION PER CC 5930(B). THE COURT DOES NOT MAKE ANY RULING AS TO WHETHER ADR COMPLIANCE IS REQUIRED REGARDING THE NEWLY ADDED DEFENDANT AS TO PREVIOUSLY ASSERTED CAUSES OF ACTION. IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO ADDRESS THIS MATTER (BASED ON GOOD FAITH AND AUTHORITY) IT MAY BE ADDRESSED ON A DEMURRER AFTER GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT TO MEET AND CONFER. THE COURT NOTES THAT OPPOSITION WAS FILED LATE WITHOUT ANY EXPLANATION. HOWEVER, SINCE IT WAS FULLY ADDRESSED IN THE REPLY, OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED BY THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: TAMARA WILLAT, CSR#4609, 415.272.1753. (501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL CARBOY, KATHRYN COFFEY'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, dismissal on file. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Zach Webster, 415.673.5600 for Defendant/Opposing party Michael Carboy.
Jonathan Patchen, 415.788.8200 for Plaintiffs/Moving parties Scott Friedman and rebecca Kaden.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN, REBECCA KADEN Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED. The Court previously denied the motion for leave to amend expressly without prejudice providing Plaintiff with opportunity to address the issues as listed in the order. Plaintiff has done so and may file an amended complaint. Davis-Sterling Act does not apply to the newly proposed causes of action per CC 5930(b). The Court does not make any ruling as to whether ADR compliance is required regarding the newly added defendant as to previously asserted causes of action. If Defendant wishes to address this matter (based on good faith and authority) it may be addressed on a demurrer after good faith attempt to meet and confer. The Court notes that opposition was filed late without any explanation. However, since it was fully addressed in the reply, opposition was considered by the Court. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Tamara Willat, CSR#4609, 415.272.1753. (501)
